prior_documents.title: Capital Requirements for Mid-Sized Banks — 2023 Final Rule
prior_documents.abstract: Final rule establishing baseline regulatory capital requirements applicable to insured depository institutions with total consolidated assets between $50 billion and $250 billion.
agencies: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
prior_documents.raw_text: This 2023 rule establishes baseline regulatory capital requirements for mid-sized banking organizations with total consolidated assets between $50 billion and $250 billion. It addresses Tier 1 capital ratios, supplementary leverage requirements, and resolution planning expectations.

enrichment.summary: Final rule from 2023 setting Tier 1 capital, supplementary leverage, and resolution planning expectations for banks $50B-$250B.
enrichment.severity_rationale: Material capital and resolution planning requirements for a broad set of mid-sized banks.

prior_documents.title: Cybersecurity Incident Notification — Banking Organizations
prior_documents.abstract: Interagency rule requiring banking organizations to notify their primary federal regulator of significant computer-security incidents within 36 hours.
prior_documents.raw_text: Banking organizations must notify their primary federal regulator of a notification incident no later than 36 hours after determining a notification incident has occurred. A notification incident is a computer-security incident that has materially disrupted or degraded, or is reasonably likely to materially disrupt or degrade, banking operations.

enrichment.summary: Interagency requirement for banks to notify regulators within 36 hours of significant computer-security incidents that materially affect operations.
enrichment.severity_rationale: Short notification window for significant cyber incidents creates real operational and reporting burden.
